description:
The 4.27 epic's stated goal — "n + 1 where ir_typer proves both operands are int compiles to one iadd" — is met inside narrow-spec bodies (`add1_s3` body is `sshr, sshr, iadd, ishl, bor`) but NOT across boundaries. Every narrow spec still takes/returns tagged FzValue i64. Untag-on-entry and retag-on-return + tagged-literal-then-untag at call sites are the residual boundary tax.

The deferral lives in two named places in src/ir_codegen.rs:

  - `build_fn_signature` (line 593+) accepts `_ft` and `_return_descr` and ignores them. Comment: "Promoting typed params (`f64` for float, raw `i64` for int) is a follow-up so it can be paired with the pre-terminator tag pass surgery it requires."

  - compile_fn native entry-param branch (line 2060+). Comment: "Native ABI passes every fz arg as tagged FzValue (i64) in .6.2.3; raw promotion is a follow-up. No raw_*_vars entry."

VR.4.2.2 closed with these deferrals; no follow-up ticket was filed. This is that ticket.

## Scope

Per narrow SpecId, drive the Cranelift Signature off the spec's per-arg Descrs and joined return Descr. Match the body's entry-param loading, the terminator coercion, and direct call sites against the chosen sig.

Mirrors the working model already shipped for the uniform/trampoline path:
  - VR.3.2 / VR.3.3 typed entry-FRAME slots (FieldKind::RawI64 / RawF64)
  - VR.3.4.1 typed result SLOT in cont frames
  - VR.3.5 / .3.5.6 typed slots in closure heap payloads
Same Descr → repr rule. Same coercion at the seam. New: it runs in REGISTERS rather than through memory.

## Invariant preserved (load-bearing)

Any-key SpecId (SpecId.0 == FnId.0) keeps its existing uniform/tagged sig. Closure/Spawn/Receive/trampoline-driven paths reach fns by FnId.0; those entry points must stay universal. Narrow specs are layered on top via direct-call resolution (already happening at resolve_callee_sid, ir_codegen.rs:2007).

## Cont stays tagged in v1 (explicit non-goal)

Continuations are still pinned to any-key SpecId at ir_codegen.rs:2260 (`cont_sid = continuation.fn_id.0`). The native-chain case (callee + cont both native) will keep one `box_int` at the seam where the narrow callee returns into the tagged cont. Lifting that requires cont narrow-spec ABI — filed as a follow-up after this lands.

## DAG (atomic, testable steps)

  STEP.1 — Descr-driven build_fn_signature.
    `build_fn_signature` reads `ft.vars[entry.params[i]]` per param. Emits raw f64 for float-only Descrs, raw i64 for int-only Descrs, tagged i64 otherwise. Same for the return based on `return_descr`. Drop the underscores. Delete the deferral comment. Test: a tiny module with `fn f(n: int) = n + 1` (proven monomorphic) produces a Cranelift signature `(i64, i64) -> i64` where the first param's AbiParam carries the right type. Unit test on `build_fn_signature` alone — no codegen.

  STEP.2 — Match native entry-param load to chosen sig.
    `compile_fn`'s `is_native` branch (ir_codegen.rs:2060) consults the sig (or, equivalently, the same ft/return_descr inputs) to populate raw_int_vars / raw_f64_vars. Block_params already get type i64 from `b.append_block_param(entry_cl, types::I64)` (line 2032) — that loop must also widen to consult the sig and append the matching type. Delete the deferral comment. Test: a narrow int spec's entry block carries `block0(v0: i64, v1: i64)` (still i64 because that's the raw int type) AND the body's first use of v0 in an iadd uses v0 directly, not `sshr_imm v0, 3`. Float case: `block0(v0: f64, v1: i64)`.

  STEP.3 — Terminator-coerce pass.
    The tag-up loop at ir_codegen.rs:2182-2197 unconditionally boxes raw vars used by the terminator. Generalize to a coerce pass that, per terminator kind, knows the receiver's expected repr per slot:
      - Return: this fn's return AbiParam type.
      - TailCall: callee sig's per-arg AbiParam types.
      - Call: callee sig's per-arg AbiParam types (cont-result repr stays as today — tagged into any-key cont).
      - Goto: keep block_param at tagged i64 (intra-fn cross-block seam stays uniform — chosen for elegance over a per-pred per-block_param re-type negotiation).
      - Halt: tagged i64 (runtime fz_halt sig unchanged).
    Coerce is a unified helper: given a Var, its current repr (tagged / raw_int / raw_f64), and a target AbiParam type, emit the right tag/untag and return the new ir::Value. Replaces the duplicate logic at terminator and at call-site arg forwarding.

  STEP.4 — Direct call sites use the coerce helper.
    Term::Call / Term::TailCall native branches (ir_codegen.rs:2244+, 2336+) replace `native_args: Vec<ir::Value> = arg_vals.clone()` with per-arg coerce against `fn_sigs[callee_sid].params[i]`. Caller's `Const::Int(n)` lowering may produce a raw `iconst.i64 n` when the receiving param is raw int (peephole; not load-bearing — `iconst.i64 N<<3|1` followed by `sshr_imm 3` cleans up at codegen later but is ugly in CLIF). Closure stubs (.29.5) at line ~2787 also coerce per their target callee sig.

  STEP.5 — Fixture-matrix bless.
    Re-bless CLIF expectations for every fixture where a narrow spec exists with non-`any` arg Descrs or return Descr. Expected diff dominated by:
      - removed `sshr_imm v, 3` at narrow spec entry uses of typed params
      - removed `ishl_imm 3 ; bor_imm 1` at narrow spec returns when return Descr is int-only
      - caller emits `iconst N` (raw) instead of `iconst (N<<3)|1` (tagged) when feeding raw-int params
      - one residual `box_int` survives at the narrow-callee → any-key-cont seam (documented; eliminated by the cont follow-up)
    JIT + AOT identical (same compile_fn). Interp unaffected (doesn't go through Cranelift sigs).

## Acceptance

  - add1.fz CLIF: `add1_s3` body is `iadd_imm v0, 1 ; <coerce-for-cont> ; return`. main passes `iconst 41` to add1_s3 (not `iconst 329`).
  - Sweep of fixture matrix — JIT/AOT bytes identical, interp unaffected.
  - All warnings green. `cargo test` green.
  - Two named deferral comments deleted; `_ft` / `_return_descr` underscores removed.
  - One commit per atom (STEP.1..STEP.5), per project discipline.

## Killset

  - Deferral comments at ir_codegen.rs:587-592 and 2063-2065 (deleted).
  - Underscored `_ft`, `_return_descr` parameters become consumed identifiers (renamed, not deleted).
  - Likely consolidation: the duplicate coerce logic at terminator + call-site collapses to one helper. Count to be measured at STEP.3/.4 commit time.

  No large dead-path falls out — tag/untag helpers (box_int, unbox_int, tagged_get, as_raw_i64, as_raw_f64) all stay live for the mixed-repr seams that remain (cont, closure stub, halt, print, any-key entry). The savings are in emitted instructions per compiled body, not in source-tree lines.

## Follow-ups (filed separately after this lands)

  - Cont narrow-spec ABI — promote cont SpecId from any-key to a narrow spec keyed on the callee's return Descr (.29.4 lifted the typer pin; codegen still pins). Eliminates the residual seam box at narrow callee → cont.
  - Any-key spec DCE — when no closure/Spawn/Receive use reaches a fn, its any-key body is dead bytes in the module. Cranelift may already DCE if not declared as a symbol; needs measurement.
  - Const-Int raw lowering peephole — emit `iconst.i64 N` directly when the consumer wants raw, instead of `iconst (N<<3)|1` followed by Cranelift folding `sshr_imm 3`.
title: VR.4 follow-up: per-Descr typed args + return in narrow-spec ABI
